danger_signals.meaning: Instant death on contact; lemming drowning hazard

danger_signals.visual_form: Tall cliff edges at bottom of screen or gaps
danger_signals.meaning: Fall damage or instant death if height exceeds safe threshold

danger_signals.visual_form: Timer display countdown; lemmings flash/explode when time reaches zero
danger_signals.meaning: All remaining lemmings explode, mission fails
Falling off screen kills lemming instantly

danger_signals.visual_form: Lemming assigned bomb skill; five-second countdown
danger_signals.meaning: Lemming self-destructs after timer, destroying nearby destructible terrain
Cannot be tunneled through by Basher, Miner, or Digger skills

safety_signals.meaning: Lemming safe zone; reaching this completes objective for that lemming

safety_signals.visual_form: Stable brown/tan rock and dirt blocks
Lemming with parachute visual above it
safety_signals.meaning: Lemming falling safely; will not take fall damage

safety_signals.visual_form: Lemming in climbing animation on vertical surface
safety_signals.meaning: Can traverse vertical obstacles; safe unless hitting overhang
Creates living barrier; other lemmings reverse direction on contact
Creates safe ascending path for other lemmings to cross obstacles

safety_signals.visual_form: Lemming actively carving through terrain in horizontal/diagonal/vertical direction
safety_signals.meaning: Creates safe passage through destructible terrain (not steel)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Anthropomorphic lemming sprite with distinctive silhouette
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Entrance trap doors (release points); exit goal structure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Trap doors open from top of screen in steady sequence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lemmings released one after another in predetermined rate; all follow each other in line

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lemmings walking left or right on solid ground with normal sprite animation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lemming ignores other lemmings (except Blockers), falls off edges, turns around when hitting unpassable obstacle

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Selected lemming highlights; skill UI shows available ability count
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lemming transitions to skill behavior (climbing, floating, building, etc.) in real-time

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lemming sprite positions near lava, cliff edge, trap, or fire zone
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lemming continues movement; no visual warning given before collision

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lemming sprite disappears or explodes; visual death effect
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lemming removed from play; death counter incremented

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Completion screen with statistics; message: 'SPOT ON' (exact %), 'to you on that attempt' (minimum + 20%)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game advances to next level or rating; remaining lemmings may continue to exit or level ends

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Level failure (time expired or save threshold not met)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Timer reaches zero; all lemmings flash and explode; 'LEVEL FAILED' state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All remaining lemmings self-destruct regardless of Bomber assignment; level restarts or returns to menu

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level names grouped by rating: 'Fun', 'Tricky', 'Taxing', 'Mayhem' (regional variants in text only)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased complexity, more hazards, stricter time limits, fewer skill uses per progression

environmental_storytelling.technique: Terrain-as-narrative: Level geometry spells words or shapes
environmental_storytelling.example: Tricky 21 (Japan): Level structure spells '666' in rocks; changed to 'ohayou' hiragana in international versions for censorship. Taxing 3 spells 'heaven' (Japan) or 'paradise' (international). Taxing 24 spells 'death' (Japan) or 'terminate' (international).
Christian crosses in dirt tileset replaced with neutral signs in US/EU versions. Christian cross in 'A Beast II of a Level' changed to generic wooden pieces in international versions.
Lava pools use warm orange/red color to signal liquid hazard; distinct from solid terrain. Traps and fire zones use visually distinctive sprites to indicate danger zones.
Difficulty categories ('Fun', 'Tricky', 'Taxing', 'Mayhem') signal escalating challenge through naming convention; visual level complexity increases correspondingly.

Level name changes between regions ('Lovely Jubilee' US vs 'Luvly Jubly' JP; 'Umbrella land' US vs 'Mary Poppins' land' JP) reflect cultural reference adaptation; 'The Other Side' (US) vs 'The Far Side' (JP) for Mayhem 12.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Walks in one direction; ignores other lemmings; falls off edges; reverses direction on hitting unpassable obstacle
Climbs vertically upward; falls if encountering overhang; allows vertical obstacle traversal
Deploys parachute during fall; descends slowly; negates fall damage
Five-second timer activates; explosion destroys nearby destructible terrain; kills the lemming; does not damage other lemmings or traps
Stands still; other lemmings reverse direction on contact; acts as living obstacle
Builds 12-step staircase in forward direction; stops if head hits ceiling, runs out of bricks, or stairway hits solid object
Carves horizontal tunnel through destructible terrain; cannot break steel barriers
Carves diagonal downward tunnel through destructible terrain; cannot break steel barriers
Carves vertical downward tunnel through destructible terrain; cannot break steel barriers
Enters exit structure; counted toward save quota; removed from level; safe
Disappears or explodes; removed from play; counted as death; cannot be saved

notes: Lemmings SNES uses minimal color-coded visual distinction due to platform constraints. Danger communication relies heavily on environmental positioning (proximity to hazards) and sprite behavioral animation rather than color warning systems. Level design geography itself is the primary storytelling medium. Regional censorship modified terrain visuals (cross replacement) and level geometry text (666, heaven, death word-spells) without altering core danger signals. Player must infer danger through spatial layout and prior experience; no explicit visual warnings precede hazards. Completion feedback uses text-based messaging ('SPOT ON', 'Right on!') with regional variation.
